recommended_activities.title: Simple Rule Game
recommended_activities.instructions: Play a short game with one rule, like rolling a ball and waiting for turns.

recommended_activities.title: Picture What/Where Game
recommended_activities.instructions: Use 3-4 pictures and ask simple what/where questions, giving a model answer first if needed.
recommended_activities.adaptation: Let the child point before speaking if speaking is hard.

retrieved_context.title: Autism profile summary
retrieved_context.text: Autism can affect social communication, flexible routines, and language development in different ways across children. Parent-friendly support often focuses on communication, shared attention, play, and predictable routines.

retrieved_context.title: What/where question rationale
retrieved_context.text: Simple picture-based WH questions support comprehension and early sentence use without needing long conversations.

retrieved_context.title: Communication choice-making rationale
retrieved_context.text: Choice-making creates repeated opportunities for a child to communicate through pointing, looking, signs, sounds, or words during a familiar daily routine.

retrieved_context.title: Global developmental delay profile summary
retrieved_context.text: Global developmental delay can involve several developmental domains at once. A short structured interview can help identify which domains need the most immediate support and what to prioritize at home.

retrieved_context.title: Word expansion rationale
retrieved_context.text: Language expansion supports early expressive language by giving the child a slightly richer version of what they already said.

full_output.safety_note: This is a non-diagnostic developmental support estimate intended to guide parent activities and follow-up questions. It is not a medical diagnosis or formal developmental assessment.

notes: Focused domains: Social / Communication, Speech / Language. Suggested activities: Simple Rule Game, Picture What/Where Game. Retrieved supporting context includes: Autism profile summary, What/where question rationale.

recommended_activities.title: Cushion Obstacle Path
recommended_activities.instructions: Make a safe path with pillows or towels for stepping over and around objects.

retrieved_context.title: Down syndrome profile summary
retrieved_context.text: Down syndrome may be associated with developmental differences across speech, motor, and adaptive skills. Helpful supports often include repetition, visual cues, and short practice embedded in daily routines.

retrieved_context.title: Global developmental delay profile summary
retrieved_context.text: Global developmental delay can involve several developmental domains at once. A short structured interview can help identify which domains need the most immediate support and what to prioritize at home.

full_output.safety_note: This is a non-diagnostic developmental support estimate intended to guide parent activities and follow-up questions. It is not a medical diagnosis or formal developmental assessment.

notes: Here is a simple home plan: Motor looks around 24-36m with confidence 0.95. Start with: Cushion Obstacle Path. Keep the activities short, playful, and repeatable.

recommended_activities.title: Pretend Play Routine
recommended_activities.instructions: Feed a doll or animal, take turns, and model one pretend action at a time.
recommended_activities.adaptation: Use real objects first, then symbolic play.

retrieved_context.title: Autism profile summary
retrieved_context.text: Autism can affect social communication, flexible routines, and language development in different ways across children. Parent-friendly support often focuses on communication, shared attention, play, and predictable routines.

retrieved_context.title: Communication choice-making rationale
retrieved_context.text: Choice-making creates repeated opportunities for a child to communicate through pointing, looking, signs, sounds, or words during a familiar daily routine.
